data.title: REG - Citigroup Global Mkt Bakkavor Group PLC - Form 8.5 (EPT/RI)
data.description:
RNS Number : 8944E Citigroup Global Markets Limited 14 April 2025  

FORM 8.5 (EPT/RI)

PUBLIC DEALING DISCLOSURE BY AN EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER WITH

RECOGNISED INTERMEDIARY STATUS DEALING IN A CLIENT-SERVING CAPACITY

Rule 8.5 of the Takeover Code (the "Code")

1. KEY INFORMATION

(a) Name of exempt principal trader:

Citigroup Global Markets Limited

(b) Name of offeror/offeree in relation to whose relevant securities this form relates:

 Use a separate form for each offeror/offeree

Bakkavor Group plc

(c) Name of the party to the offer with which exempt principal trader is connected:

Bakkavor Group plc

(d) Date dealing undertaken:

11 April 2025

(e) In addition to the company in 1(b) above, is the exempt principal trader making disclosures in respect of any other party to this offer?

 If it is a cash offer or possible cash offer, state "N/A"

Greencore Group plc

2. DEALINGS BY THE EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER

Where there have been dealings in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(b), copy table 2(a), (b), (c) or (d) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security dealt in.

The currency of all prices and other monetary amounts should be stated.

(a) Purchases and sales

Class of relevant security

2p ordinary

Purchases/ sales

Purchase

Total number of securities

5,056

Highest price per unit paid/received

GBP 1.7500

Lowest price per unit paid/received

GBP 1.7500

(b) Cash-settled derivative transactions

Class of relevant security

2p ordinary

Product description

 e.g. CFD

TRS

Nature of dealing

 e.g. opening/closing a long/short position, increasing/reducing a long/short position

Increasing Short Position

Number of reference securities

5,056

Price per unit

GBP 1.7500

(c) Stock-settled derivative transactions (including options)

(i) Writing, selling, purchasing or varying

  • Class of relevant security

  • Product description

     e.g. call option

  • Writing, purchasing, selling, varying etc.

  • Number of securities to which option relates

  • Exercise price per unit

  • Type

     e.g. American, European etc.

  • Expiry date

  • Option money paid/ received per unit

(ii) Exercise

  • Class of relevant security

  • Product description

     e.g. call option

  • Exercising/ exercised against

  • Number of securities

  • Exercise price per unit

(d) Other dealings (including subscribing for new securities)

  • Class of relevant security

  • Nature of dealing

     e.g. subscription, conversion

  • Details

  • Price per unit (if applicable)

3. OTHER INFORMATION

(a) Indemnity and other dealing arrangements

  • Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:

     Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included. If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • None

(b) Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives

  • Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any other person relating to:

     (i) the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or

     (ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:

     If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • None

Date of disclosure:                            

14 April 2025

Contact name:

Craig Watson

Telephone number:

02890 409 605

Public disclosures under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service and must also be emailed to the Takeover Panel at monitoring@disclosure.org.uk. The Panels Market Surveillance Unit is available for consultation in relation to the Codes dealing disclosure requirements on +44 (0)20 7638 0129.

The Code can be viewed on the Panels website at www.thetakeoverpanel.org.uk.

data.title: Rapeseed Hits 6-week High
data.description: Rapeseed increased to a 6-week high of 528.50 EUR/T. Over the past 4 weeks, Rapeseed gained 13.17%, and in the last 12 months, it increased 14.46%.

data.title: South Korean Negotiating Team to Visit U.S. Soon for Talks, Acting President Says
RCS - Alba Mineral Resrcs. - Commencement of Second Gold Coin Auction
data.description:
RNS Number : 8948E Alba Mineral Resources PLC 14 April 2025  

Alba Mineral Resources Plc / EPIC: ALBA / Market: AIM / Sector: Mining

14 April 2025

Alba Mineral Resources Plc

("Alba" or the "Company") 

Clogau Update

Commencement of Second Gold Coin Auction

Alba Mineral Resources Plc (AIM: ALBA) is pleased to confirm that, following the successful sale of the first of its limited edition 1oz Welsh gold coins, the second coin auction has now commenced.

Alba has minted just three exclusive 24-carat, 1-ounce "Tyn-y-Cornel" gold coins, each of which has been made entirely from gold extracted by the Company from the Clogau-St David's Gold Mine in Wales ("Clogau" or the "Mine"). The auction for the first of these coins closed on 3 April 2025 with the hammer price reaching £20,000, a premium of almost 8.5 times the then spot (or market) price of gold.

The auction will close this Wednesday, 16 April, at 5pm BST, unless the "buy-it now" price is met before that time. Collectors and investors are invited to register for the auction at https://alba.irostrum.auction/.

Figure 1: The front of the coin features a striking Welsh dragon atop Cadair Idris, one of Snowdonia's (Eryri's) most famous mountains. Background: specimen of quartz containing visible gold from Clogau-St David's (not included in the auction lot).

Figure 2: The reverse of the coin pays homage to the Mine's golden era with an image inspired by historic photographs of miners outside the Tyn-y-Cornel adit.  Background: specimen of quartz containing visible gold from Clogau-St David's (not included in the auction lot).

The coin will be presented in a presentation case and accompanied by a numbered certificate of authenticity.

George Frangeskides, Executive Chairman, commented:

"We are delighted to announce the commencement of the second public auction of our beautifully designed and crafted limited edition 1-ounce pure Welsh gold coins. This is the first gold production to come out of the historic Clogau-St David's Gold Mine in more than 25 years. The first coin fetched an impressive price at auction, testament to the enduring and exceptional appeal of gold derived from the Clogau Gold Mine. We look forward to following the progress of bidding until the hammer falls this Wednesday evening."

**ENDS**

Engage with Alba by asking questions, watching video summaries and reading what other shareholders have to say. Navigate to our interactive Investor Hub here:

https://albamineralresources.com/link/oPBWly

For further information, please visit the Alba Mineral Resources plc investor website (www.albamineralresources.com) and sign up to receive news and engage with the Alba management team. Subscribe to our news alert service (https://alba-l.investorhub.com/auth/signup) and visit @AlbaMinerals on X (formerly Twitter).

Contact Details

Alba Mineral Resources plc, George Frangeskides, Executive Chairman

Tel: +44 20 3950 0725 

The GingerBread Agency, press enquiries

Gaby@gingerbreadagency.com / Tel: +44 7808 472 139

Madelaine@gingerbreadagency.com

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Monday at 5 AM ET
data.description:
Global Markets Rise on Tech Tariff Reprieve

Stocks gained after Trump exempted semiconductor equipment, smartphones and other electronics from tariffs, even though officials later signaled they would be put in their own levy bracket.

German Exports to U.S. Hit Highest in Two Decades

The uptick could be short-lived given the Trump administration's raising of tariff barriers on imports from Europe.

U.S. Allies Are Sitting Out Trump's Trade War With China

Imposition of new tariffs on nearly every country adds to concerns in Europe and Asia about relying on America.

Tariff Upheaval Threatens Private Equity's Exit Recovery Hopes

U.S. private-equity exits and their total value rose in the first quarter compared with a year ago, but recent market volatility puts that momentum at risk.

China's Exports Surge as Orders Front-Loaded Before Tariffs

China's exports surged in March as shipments were likely front-loaded before President Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs, but they are expected to falter in the coming months.

Shifting Signals on Tech Tariffs Fuel Fresh Trade Uncertainty

Various comments from the Trump administration over the weekend regarding tariffs on consumer electronics hint at another chaotic week on Wall Street and in Washington.

Another Earnings Season Is Here. Why Results Don't Matter as Much This Time Around.

Investors are more eager to parse management teams' tones about a potential downturn in their forecasts than first-quarter earnings.

Will the Last Investor to Leave America Please Turn Out the Lights

When market veterans gather, the talk often turns to memorable crashes. Last week should join the list.

Wall Street's 'Smart Money' Braced for Tariff Chaos. It Was Still Caught Off Guard.

Even hedge funds weren't prepared for the gyrations in stocks, Treasurys and currencies.

So What, Exactly, Is a Recession Anyway?

No matter how bad things feel, don't expect a recession call anytime soon-at least not from the economics group that is the established arbiter.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Monday at 5 AM ET
data.description:
Tesla Stock Rises. Earnings Expectations Are 'Low' but There Is Some Hope.

There could be one reason for optimism when the EV maker posts first-quarter numbers, says RBC analyst Tom Narayan.

Asian Tech Stocks, Apple Suppliers Rise After U.S. Tariff Reprieve

Asian tech hardware stocks broadly rose after the Trump administration exempted tariffs on semiconductor equipment, smartphones and other electronics, despite administration officials saying that these tech products would face their own levies.

Will more people ride out a trade war with Netflix? The streamer's results will offer clues.

With quarterly results due Thursday, analysts will be looking for signs of indirect impacts of tariffs on the streaming platform.

Tim Cook's 'Long Arc of Time' Prepared Apple for the Trade War

The CEO managed to help the iPhone avoid another U.S. battle with China.

Amazon Wants to Keep Prices Low. One Seller's Tariff Woes Show Why It's Hard

If tariffs on Chinese products stay high, Ramon Gonzalez said he might have to close his online shop.

Nvidia's Dominance Reflects the CEO's Unique Business Logic

The AI powerhouse has become ubiquitous, paradoxically by doing only what it deems essential.

Europe Scrambles to Break Its Dependence on Musk's Satellites

Authorities in the region are counting on a homegrown alternative to Starlink and its unpredictable owner.

Ireland's Privacy Watchdog Probes Musk's Grok AI Model

Ireland's data privacy watchdog said it is looking into whether Grok has been illegally trained on European user posts on Musk's X social media platform.

Temasek-Backed Private-Equity Firm Raises Over $600 Mln in Latest Funding Round

Private-equity firm ABC Impact has raised over $600.0 million in its latest funding round, drawing interest from investors, including a multilateral development bank and a sovereign-wealth fund.

Hollywood Is Cranking Out Original Movies. Audiences Aren't Showing Up.

New movies based on fresh ideas are fizzling at the box office.

data.title: Daiwa Securities Group Issues 35 Billion Yen in Hybrid Bonds
data.description:
Daiwa Securities Group is issuing 35 billion yen hybrid bonds at an initial interest rate of 1.902% per annum, according to its bourse filing last Friday.

The bonds, maturing on Oct. 30, 2060, allow optional interest deferral and early redemption starting Oct. 30, 2030, with semi-annual payments on April 30 and Oct. 30.

Jointly managed by major securities firms, the public offering for the bonds will close on April 30.

data.title: Tertiary Minerals Intersects Copper, Nickel Mineralization in Zambia
data.description:
Tertiary Minerals said Monday an initial scout drilling program at the Mukai copper project in Zambia intersected near surface copper and nickel mineralization.

The program drilled three holes for a total length of 554 meters at the project, which is under agreement with First Quantum Minerals.

The mineral explorer and developer will review the next steps of the exploration program.

data.title: Euro at Over 3-Year High
data.description:
The euro hovered near the $1.14 mark, its strongest level since late January 2022, after posting a 3.6% weekly gain.

The rally was driven by mounting global trade tensions and growing uncertainty over U.S. tariff policy, which have reignited recession fears and dented investor confidence in U.S. assets.

Over the weekend, U.S. President Trump granted temporary tariff exclusions on smartphones and computers imported from China, while signaling that new levies on semiconductors would be announced later this week, with a final decision on phone tariffs still pending.

The developments come amid ongoing volatility and mixed messaging around trade policy.

Looking ahead, the European Central Bank is scheduled to hold its policy meeting on Thursday, with markets broadly expecting a 25 basis point rate cut.

Investors will be closely watching for commentary from ECB officials on the impact of trade tensions on the Eurozone economy and the future path of interest rates.

data.title: Stressed market conditions in derivatives on OMXS30, OMXO20 and OMXC25 (123/25)
data.description:
NASDAQ NordicProduct information

Stressed market conditions in derivatives on OMXS30, OMXO20 and OMXC25 (123/25)

Nasdaq has decided to call for stressed market conditions in derivatives on OMXStockholm 30 (OMXS30), OMX Oslo 20 (OMXO20) and OMX Copenhagen 25 (OMXC25)until close of business as of April 14, 2025.

Stressed market conditions in derivatives is called for when the underlyingasset to a derivative experience high and short term intraday volatility orwhen other factors causes high uncertainty in the pricing of derivatives. Whenstressed market is called for it means that market makers are allowed to quoteprices with double the normal spread.

For contact details please see attached file.https://view.news.eu.nasdaq.com/view?id=b3dff122009788de780a88bb3ba502d7b&lang=en&src=omxlink
